levels.title: The Dark Room
levels.narrative: You boot up the ancient server. The screen flickers to life with a blinking cursor. You find yourself in an unknown location on this forgotten machine. Before you can explore, you need to figure out where you are.
levels.riddle:
Where am I? Lost in the machine's maze,
A single command will clear the haze.
It prints your path, your working ground —
Type it now, and you'll be found.

files.welcome.txt: Welcome to the Lost Server. Your journey begins here.

hints: You need to find out your current location in the filesystem.
The command stands for 'print working directory'.

levels.title: Looking Around
levels.narrative: Good, you know where you are now. But what surrounds you? The server's directories hold many secrets. Time to look around and see what files and folders are here.
levels.riddle:
Eyes wide open, take a look,
What's around you in this nook?
Two small letters, that's the key —
List the contents for all to see.

files.readme.txt: If you're reading this, you've found the Lost Server. Good luck.

hints: You need to see what files and folders exist here.
The command is just two letters — it 'lists' things.

levels.title: The Hidden Chamber
levels.narrative: The server seems straightforward at first glance. But in the world of Unix, not everything is as it appears. Some files hide in plain sight, their names beginning with a dot. The phantom_admin surely left hidden messages...
levels.riddle:
I am here but unseen, a dot before my name.
The normal listing won't reveal my hidden game.
Add a flag to show me, small as can be —
Discover what's concealed, and set the secret free.

files.notes.txt: Nothing important here... or is there?
files.todo.txt:
1. Hide the secrets
2. Lock the vault
3. Disappear
files..secret_config: phantom_admin was here. Follow the trail.

hints: Some files start with a dot (.) and are hidden from normal listing.
The ls command has a flag that shows ALL files, including hidden ones.

levels.title: Going Deeper
levels.narrative: You've found a hidden message! It mentions something about a vault deep inside the server. You notice a directory called 'vault' — perhaps the next clue is inside. Time to learn how to move between directories.
levels.riddle:
A door stands before you, a folder in disguise.
Step through it now — change directory, be wise.
Inside the vault, a treasure file awaits,
Read its name to unlock the gates.

files.map.txt: The vault is nearby. Enter it to find the next clue.
files.vault/decoy.txt: This is not the file you're looking for.

hints: You need to enter the 'vault' directory to see what's inside.
Use 'cd' followed by the directory name to change into it, then list files.

levels.title: The Way Back
levels.narrative: Nice work! But you've gone deep into the vault. Now you need to go back up to continue exploring. Every good explorer knows how to retrace their steps.
levels.riddle:
You've gone too deep, now climb back out,
Two dots will help, without a doubt.
Return to where you started from,
And list the files — your journey's not done.

files.home_marker.txt: You made it back! Well done, explorer.

hints: You need to go UP in the directory tree, back to where you started.
'cd ..' moves you one directory up. 'cd ~' takes you home.
If you're deep inside, try: cd ~ then ls
